title: Patriots might have shot at top free-agent receiver after Colts’ franchise-tag gamble
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Colts opted for a transition tag on QB Daniel Jones, making WR Alec Pierce available in free agency.
Alec Pierce, 25, led the NFL in yards per catch in 2024 and 2025 and recorded 1,003 yards in 2025.
Pierce could command $25-30 million annually, fitting into the Patriots' $39.2 million cap space if they restructure contracts.

title: Vermont postal worker allegedly threw away mail she was supposed to deliver for months
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Natasha Morisseau, a Vermont postal worker, was cited for allegedly discarding mail instead of delivering it.
Police found opened and unopened packages and holiday cards in a dumpster and Morisseau's mail vehicle.
Morisseau was suspended by USPS and is scheduled to appear in Vermont Superior Court on March 17.

title: Man in his 80s, dog dead in Foxborough fire
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A man in his 80s and his dog died in a house fire on Granite Street in Foxborough.
The Foxborough Fire Department responded to the fire around 3 a.m. after police detected smoke.
Firefighters from Foxborough, Mansfield, and Plainville extinguished the fire, but the house sustained major damage.

title: Patriots players brought strippers, hookahs to celebrate at a Boston restaurant, its owner says. He’s now facing a license violation.
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Estella's in Boston faces a liquor license violation due to a party involving Patriots players with strippers and hookahs.
Patriots players allegedly brought hookahs, alcohol, and cash to the restaurant, which was open past the legal 2 a.m. closing time.
Restaurant owner Helder Brandao claims players' activities were unplanned and is considering legal action after the incident.

title: Mass. students place No. 1 nationwide for AP scores, break all-time record
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts students achieved the highest nationwide scores on AP exams, with 35.8% scoring 3 or higher.
Massachusetts had the highest percentage of Black students taking AP exams, excluding Washington D.C.
The state subsidized over 90,783 AP exams for nearly 50,000 low-income students as part of educational initiatives.

title: ‘Forced to take this pay raise’: Quincy City Councilors are collecting the raise they promised to repeal
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: New Quincy City Council members, elected on anti-raise platforms, must accept 50% salary increases due to legal constraints.
Several councilors plan to donate their raises to nonprofits, despite campaigning against the salary increases.
Quincy City Council working to send repeal of mayor and council raises to the Ordinance Committee for discussion.

title: Rosenthal: Red Sox remain the ‘best fit’ as a trade suitor for Isaac Paredes
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox remain interested in Astros' Isaac Paredes, potentially trading Jarren Duran as part of the deal.
Boston previously acquired Caleb Durbin to fill infield gaps, setting up a conflict if Paredes joins.
Trade focus on Paredes could impact the Red Sox's lineup flexibility, notably the DH and outfield positions.

title: New ESPN mock draft has Patriots choosing a ‘pass-rushing menace’ in first round
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: ESPN's mock draft by Jordan Reid predicts Patriots will pick Texas A&M's Cashius Howell, a pass-rushing specialist, at No. 31.
Howell recorded 11.5 sacks in the 2025 season, ranking seventh among FBS defensive linemen, and topped SEC with 41 pressures.
Concerns for Howell include his smaller frame and potential struggles as a three-down NFL player; potential designated rusher role.

title: Salem State student’s killer convicted 40 years after her murder, prosecutors say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: John Carey, 66, was convicted of murdering Salem State student Claire Gravel in 1986, nearly 40 years after the crime.
Evidence linking Carey to the murder was found in 2012, including DNA on a tank top used to strangle Gravel.
Carey, already serving time for attempted murder, will be sentenced to life without parole on March 26 for Gravel's murder.

title: N.H. woman accused of civil rights violation after allegedly shooting at lost man because he was Black
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Diane Durgin, 67, is charged with shooting at a Black man who mistakenly drove to her property in Weare, N.H.
Durgin faces up to a $5,000 fine for each alleged civil rights violation and is charged with criminal threatening and attempted assault.
Prosecutors requested a preliminary injunction to prevent Durgin from contacting the victim and repeating her alleged behavior.
